name: Classic Ny Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Crust: Combine all crust ingredients in a food processor and blend until well mixed. Press into the bottom and half up the sides of a 10‑inch springform pan.
2. Filling: In a large mixing bowl, beat cream cheese with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Gradually add sugar, flour, lemon peel, salt, vanilla, eggs, and yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Scrape down the sides of the bowl once or twice. Stir in sour cream; the mixture will be thin. Pour into the prepared crust lined pan.
3. Place on a baking sheet. Bake at 500°F (260°C) for 8 minutes or until the top is golden. Reduce temperature to 200°F (93°C) and continue baking for 1 hour or until the center jiggles slightly. Remove from oven and let cool in a draft-free place at room temperature for 3 hours or overnight.
4. Remove sides of springform. Make desired glaze by blending strawberries, water, sugar, cornstarch, and red food coloring as directed. Spread evenly over the top of the cheesecake.
5. Refrigerate until serving. Slice remaining berries in half and arrange in concentric circles over the top. Spoon warm glaze over the berries, letting some drip down the sides. Refrigerate until set, about 1 hour.
